compiled_state.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can safely re-invoke implementation-plan on an epic with existing children
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implementation-plan gains a re-invocation guard at entry that reads the existing ticket graph and produces a diff plan rather than a full plan when children already exist.
**Why**: Today, re-invoking implementation-plan on an epic/story with children creates duplicate tasks and has no awareness of closed work. This makes re-planning unsafe and forces manual intervention.
**Scope**:
- IN: Re-invocation guard logic in implementation-plan entry, child classification (closed/in-progress/open), archived ticket handling (--include-archived), diff plan output (new + reopened tasks only)
- OUT: Sprint detection logic (separate epic 9d3e-957d), brainstorm escalation signal (Story 2), cascade orchestration (Story 3)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, invoking implementation-plan on an epic that already has closed child tasks produces only new or reopened tasks — closed tasks are never modified, duplicated, or reopened
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation-plan safely handles re-invocation with an in-flight ticket graph"
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan reads archived children via --include-archived and classifies them as closed (read-only), preventing re-creation of archived completed work
  ← Satisfies: "reads all children (including archived via --include-archived)"
- When this story is complete, in-progress children are flagged for review in the diff plan output rather than silently overwritten or duplicated
  ← Satisfies: "classifies each as closed (read-only), in-progress (flagged for review), or open (candidate for revision)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Reliability] Incorrect child classification could duplicate tasks or lose completed work — high-stakes correctness
- [Testing] Needs behavioral tests with mock ticket graphs (closed, in-progress, open, archived states)
- [Maintainability] "Closed = read-only" invariant must be documented and enforced consistently

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
